# Open the provided file and read raw text
path = "/mnt/data/file-SUXHtn7nxENu7fsLCgCFqU-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    raw = f.read()
raw[:500], len(raw)

import re, json, textwrap

raw = open("/mnt/data/file-SUXHtn7nxENu7fsLCgCFqU-text_to_analyse.txt","r",encoding="utf-8").read()

spans = []

# 1) Ayahs between (aya_start) and (aya_end)
for m in re.finditer(r"\(aya_start\)(.*?)\(aya_end\)", raw, re.DOTALL):
    text = m.group(1)
    # Trim edge whitespace/punct per rule
    trim = text.strip(' \n\t\r"\'«»()[]{}<>\u200f\u200e،؛:.,!؟…ـ')
    # But we need indices of the trimmed in the raw
    start = raw.find(trim, m.start(1))
    end = start + len(trim)
    assert raw[start:end] == trim
    spans.append((start,end,trim,'Aya'))

# 2) Hadith phrase "إنما بعثت معلما"
hadith_phrase = "إنما بعثت معلما"
for m in re.finditer(re.escape(hadith_phrase), raw):
    # Check local cue within ±64 chars for hadith cues
    # In this text it is preceded by 'قال النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم' and followed by رواه
    # We'll accept all occurrences since they occur in the main list
    spans.append((m.start(), m.end(), hadith_phrase, 'Hadith'))

# 3) References section ayat with "كقوله تعالى", "كما في قوله تعالى"
# We'll search patterns after those cues until before surah mention 'غافر:' etc.
# But better to explicitly extract the known fragments as they appear.

refs_texts = [
"وَقالَ الَّذِي آمَنَ يا قَوْمِ اتَّبِعُونِ أَهْدِكُمْ سَبِيلَ الرَّشادِ* يا قَوْمِ إِنَّما هذِهِ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا مَتاعٌ وَإِنَّ الْآخِرَةَ هِيَ دارُ الْقَرارِ",
"يا أَبَتِ إِنِّي رَأَيْتُ أَحَدَ عَشَرَ كَوْكَباً وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ رَأَيْتُهُمْ لِي ساجِدِينَ",
"وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ الْبَناتِ سُبْحانَهُ وَلَهُمْ ما يَشْتَهُونَ",
"فَلا أُقْسِمُ بِمَواقِعِ النُّجُومِ* وَإِنَّهُ لَقَسَمٌ لَوْ تَعْلَمُونَ عَظِيمٌ"
]
for t in refs_texts:
    idx = raw.find(t)
    if idx != -1:
        spans.append((idx, idx+len(t), t, 'Aya'))
    else:
        print("Not found:", t)

# Sort by start
spans_sorted = sorted(spans, key=lambda x: x[0])
spans_sorted, len(spans_sorted)
